results.title: Evidence-Based Intelligent Diagnostic and Therapeutic Visualization System with Large Language Models: Multi-Turn Interaction and Multimodal Treatment Plan Generation
results.problem: Opaque reasoning processes and passive interaction in AI-assisted traditional Chinese medicine diagnostic tools hinder effective syndrome differentiation and treatment.
results.solution: A knowledge-enhanced visual diagnostic system with a Neo4j knowledge graph, four-stage symptom matching pipeline, and multimodal treatment presentation
results.technology: Knowledge graph-driven visualization and multimodal interaction utilizing large language models, artificial intelligence-generated illustrations, three-dimensional meridian-acupoint models, and evidence-based literature.
results.impact: The proposed system enhances the transparency and interpretability of traditional Chinese medicine diagnostic reasoning and treatment plans, improving diagnostic trust and reducing cognitive load among clinicians and patients.

results.title: Detective scaffolding for within-session reasoning development: a three-phase framework evaluated in polymer engineering and pre-university outreach
results.problem: Detecting root causes of industrial defects in engineering students
results.solution: Detective scaffolding framework with three-phase instructional sequence
results.technology: Evidence-Centred Design instrument using staged in-class polls as designed evidence probes
results.impact: Engineering students improve root cause identification rates with enhanced data-analysis confidence and AI explanation ability.
results.summary: The paper presents a detective scaffolding framework that drives reasoning convergence in engineering students, suggesting portability across disciplines and educational levels.

results.title: Academic Integrity and Emotional Responses to Inappropriate LLM Use in Software Engineering Education
results.problem: The emotional responses of software engineering students to using large language models (LLMs) in ways perceived as academically inappropriate, highlighting the need for guidance on acceptable assistance and authorship.
results.solution: A framework for teaching students about the risks and benefits of LLM use in software engineering education, including moral discomfort and concern about penalties.
results.technology: The proposed framework incorporates a socio-technical approach to address the complex relationships between students' emotional experiences, institutional practices, and performance pressures.
results.impact: This study provides insights into the emotional experiences of software engineering students using LLMs in ways perceived as academically inappropriate, informing the development of guidance on acceptable assistance and authorship.
